sources.author: Swyx
sources.headline: Founder at SmythOS | Ex-Amazon AWS | AI Engineering Evangelist
sources.post_summary: Discusses the evolution of AI agents from hype to practical deployment, highlighting key frameworks like LangChain and AutoGen for building reliable agentic systems. Shares insights on overcoming challenges in multi-agent collaboration and real-world automation use cases. Emphasizes the need for robust developer tools to scale AI infrastructure.

sources.author: Andrej Karpathy
sources.headline: Founder at Eureka Labs | Former Director of AI at Tesla
sources.post_summary: Explores the future of developer tools powered by AI agents, predicting a shift where agents handle 80% of coding tasks. Details infrastructure requirements for training and deploying these agents at scale. Warns about the automation of software engineering jobs while advocating for upskilling.

sources.author: Nat Friedman
sources.headline: Co-Founder at GitHub (former) | AI Investor
sources.post_summary: Breaks down recent advancements in AI infrastructure for agentic workflows, spotlighting open-source tools like CrewAI for automation pipelines. Analyzes cost reductions in running agent swarms and their impact on enterprise adoption. Calls for standardized protocols in multi-agent systems.

sources.author: Hamel Husain
sources.headline: Independent AI Researcher | Ex-GitHub
sources.post_summary: Critiques current AI agent frameworks and proposes improvements in developer tools for better error handling and observability. Shares a case study on automating data pipelines with agents, reducing manual dev time by 70%. Discusses infrastructure bottlenecks in production deployments.

sources.author: Sarah Guo
sources.headline: Founder & General Partner at Conviction Partners
sources.post_summary: Highlights trending AI infrastructure startups enabling agent orchestration at scale. Predicts explosion in automation tools for devs, with agents becoming the new API layer. References recent benchmarks showing superior performance in agent-based systems.

sources.author: Sami Rahman
sources.headline: Founder @ RevGenius | GTM Engineering Expert
sources.post_summary: Discusses building scalable GTM engines by integrating engineering principles into go-to-market strategies. Shares a framework for aligning product, sales, and marketing through data-driven automation. Emphasizes measuring GTM velocity as a key metric for revenue growth.

sources.author: Josh Braun
sources.headline: Sales Coach & Outbound Expert
sources.post_summary: Breaks down effective outbound automation without sounding spammy. Advises on personalized sequences using AI tools while maintaining human touch. Includes case study showing 3x reply rates from refined automation setups.

sources.author: Briana McNally
sources.headline: Head of Sales Operations @ Gong
sources.post_summary: Explores the evolution of sales ops into revenue operations, highlighting tools for cross-functional alignment. Shares playbook for ops teams to drive efficiency in forecasting and deal velocity. Stresses the need for ops leaders to think like engineers.

sources.author: Ann Handley
sources.headline: Chief Content Officer @ MarketingProfs
sources.post_summary: Outlines a content strategy framework tailored for B2B revenue teams. Focuses on creating assets that support GTM motions like outbound and ABM. Provides examples of content repurposing to boost sales enablement.

sources.author: Dan Tirella
sources.headline: RevOps Leader @ HubSpot
sources.post_summary: Details how revenue operations can leverage automation for outbound scaling. Covers tech stack recommendations and common pitfalls in sales ops automation. Shares metrics from a recent implementation that cut ramp time by 40%.

sources.author: Arvid Kahl
sources.headline: Serial Entrepreneur, Author of Zero to Sold & The Embedded Entrepreneur
sources.post_summary: Arvid shares his experience shipping his latest SaaS product as a solo founder, detailing the tech stack, challenges overcome, and initial user feedback. He emphasizes the importance of building in public to gain early traction and iterate quickly. The post includes a launch announcement and call for beta testers.

sources.author: KP
sources.headline: Indie Hacker & Solo Developer
sources.post_summary: KP recounts pivoting from a corporate marketing role to full-time solo development, launching his first app after 6 months of self-taught coding. He breaks down the revenue numbers from week one and lessons on validating ideas publicly. Encourages others considering tech career shifts.

sources.author: Pieter Levels
sources.headline: Founder of Nomad List, Remote OK, Hoodmaps
sources.post_summary: Pieter updates his audience on shipping a new feature to one of his bootstrapped products while working solo across multiple projects. He discusses time management hacks and the benefits of transparent progress sharing on LinkedIn. Includes metrics on user growth post-launch.

sources.author: Sahil Lavingia
sources.headline: Founder & CEO at Gumroad
sources.post_summary: Sahil reflects on his career pivot from Pinterest to building Gumroad solo initially, sharing how public documentation accelerated product development and community building. He advises aspiring builders on starting small and iterating via public feedback loops.

sources.author: Daniel Vassallo
sources.headline: Ex-AWS, Indie Maker, Small Bets Advocate
sources.post_summary: Daniel details shipping a micro-SaaS product as a solo dev post-AWS career pivot, with honest revenue shares and build logs. He highlights the freedom of independent building and tips for others transitioning to tech entrepreneurship.
